items.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
items.summary: This post describes how TGS achieved near-linear scaling for distributed training and expanded context windows for their Vision Transformer-based SFM using Amazon SageMaker HyperPod. This joint solution cut training time from 6 months to just 5 days while enabling analysis of seismic volumes larger than previously possible.

items.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
items.summary: In this post, we show you how to configure AWS Network Firewall to restrict AgentCore resources to an allowlist of approved internet domains. This post focuses on domain-level filtering using SNI inspection — the first layer of a defense-in-depth approach.

items.title: Rocket Close transforms mortgage document processing with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon Textract
items.summary: Through a strategic partnership with the AWS Generative AI Innovation Center (GenAIIC), Rocket Close developed an intelligent document processing solution that has significantly reduced processing time, making the process 15 times faster. The solution, which uses Amazon Textract for OCR processing and Amazon Bedrock for foundation models (FMs), achieves a strong 90% overall accuracy in document segmentation, classification, and field extraction.

items.title: ADeLe: Predicting and explaining AI performance across tasks
items.summary:
AI benchmarks report how large language models (LLMs) perform on specific tasks but provide little insight into their underlying capabilities that drive their performance. They do not explain failures or reliably predict outcomes on new tasks. To address this, Microsoft researchers in collaboration with Princeton University and Universitat Politècnica de València introduce ADeLe (opens in new tab) (AI […]
The post ADeLe: Predicting and explaining AI performance across tasks appeared first on Microsoft Research.

notable.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
notable.summary: TGS achieved near-linear scaling for distributed training of a Vision Transformer-based seismic foundation model, reducing training time from 6 months to 5 days and enabling analysis of larger seismic volumes.
notable.notability_reason: This item describes a significant advancement in distributed training for large models, achieving near-linear scaling and drastically reducing training time, which is highly relevant for practitioners in the field.

notable.title: Rocket Close transforms mortgage document processing with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon Textract
notable.summary: Rocket Close's solution, using Amazon Textract and Bedrock, reduces mortgage document processing time by 15 times and achieves 90% accuracy in segmentation and classification.
notable.notability_reason: The development of an intelligent document processing solution that significantly improves processing speed and accuracy represents a notable advancement in the application of classification and segmentation techniques.

notable.title: ADeLe: Predicting and explaining AI performance across tasks
notable.summary: Microsoft researchers introduced ADeLe, a framework aimed at predicting and explaining the performance of large language models across various tasks, addressing limitations in current AI benchmarks.
notable.notability_reason: ADeLe introduces a new framework for predicting and explaining AI performance across tasks, which is a significant advancement in understanding LLM capabilities and improving their application.
